mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing the present value of expected corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.5 percent, it signals a structural stabilization of the labor market that reduces recession risk premiums and supports consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio for the threshold strategy is negative over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized recovery in consumer confidence and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization rebound and supply-side easing, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations during post-shock recovery periods, or the autocorrelation of the signal exceeds 0.8 indicating pure noise.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter increase in real gross domestic product confirms aggregate demand expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate profit growth expectations and sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the GDP growth signal remains below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows, or 60-day forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth direction (Spearman rho < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index level drops below 210, it signals deflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain ultra-accommodative policy, reducing real discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The threshold strategy yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 58 percent of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains below 210 for consecutive periods.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate remains above 7.0 percent indicates labor force re-entry and early-cycle job creation, which reduces tail-risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a pooled OLS regression (p > 0.15), or the strategy underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 100 basis points annually over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production exceeds 102.0, it confirms that manufacturing capacity has surpassed pre-recession baselines, triggering profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon due to valuation compression.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day forward returns in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40 percent over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.
